name: Egnyte Content Collaboration API
what_it_does: Egnyte Content Collaboration and Governance REST API for enterprise file management, data governance, and compliance-focused content collaboration. Enables AI agents to manage file and folder operations for enterprise content management automation, handle permission and access control management for data governance workflow, access content classification and sensitive data discovery for compliance automation, retrieve audit log and activity tracking for enterprise security monitoring, manage link sharing and external collaboration for secure file distribution, handle version control and file history for document lifecycle management, access life sciences validation and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance workflow, retrieve construction project file management and BIM document control, manage HIPAA-compliant PHI file storage and access logging, and integrate Egnyte content data with construction ERP, clinical systems, and business applications.
use_cases: Managing construction project documents from project agents
Enforcing data governance from compliance monitoring agents
Controlling PHI access from HIPAA compliance agents
Auditing file access from security monitoring agents
not_for: Consumer file sharing without enterprise compliance requirements
Organizations using SharePoint or Box exclusively
Simple file storage without governance and compliance needs
best_when: A life sciences, construction, or regulated industry company using Egnyte wants AI agents to automate document management, access control, compliance monitoring, sensitive data discovery, and audit logging.
avoid_when: HIPAA PHI STORAGE RISK: Egnyte stores PHI for healthcare customers — automated file operations on PHI require BAA, access logging, and minimum-necessary access principles; automated bulk PHI access or transfer creates HIPAA breach risk. 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records — life sciences Egnyte deployments for validated systems (clinical, manufacturing) require electronic record and signature validation; automated file creation or modification outside validation workflow creates 21 CFR Part 11 compliance issues. Data classification errors — automated sensitive data classification and labeling errors can result in over-sharing of confidential files or under-protecting regulated data.

auth.notes: Egnyte uses OAuth 2.0. Developer portal at developers.egnyte.com. Webhooks for file events. REST API with JSON. Mountain View, California HQ. Founded 2007 by Vineet Jain. Private (ClearBridge Investments, IGSB, GV). $1.6B+ valuation. 17,000+ business customers. Strong in life sciences, construction, and regulated industries. Competes with Box, SharePoint, and Procore for construction and life sciences document management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual per-user subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription tiers; Business, Enterprise, and Government plans
pricing.notes: Mountain View, CA. Founded 2007. ClearBridge/IGSB/GV-backed. $1.6B+ valuation. 17,000+ business customers. Life sciences and construction focus. Competes with Box and SharePoint.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: HIPAA PHI AUTOMATED ACCESS: Automated file operations on PHI-containing folders require BAA, access logging, and minimum-necessary access; automated bulk PHI download or transfer creates HIPAA breach notification risk

21 CFR Part 11 GxP validation — life sciences Egnyte deployments for validated systems require electronic record and signature validation; automated file creation outside validated change control workflow creates 21 CFR Part 11 compliance issues

Data classification accuracy — automated sensitive data classification must have high accuracy; false negative classification (missing PII/PHI label) creates data governance gap; false positive (over-classifying) creates operational friction

Permission inheritance complexity — Egnyte folder permission inheritance has complex rules; automated permission changes at parent folders must consider downstream access impact on subfolders and files
Webhook retry handling — Egnyte webhooks require idempotent processing; webhook retry on delivery failure can create duplicate processing in automated workflows without deduplication logic
Rate limit throttling — Egnyte API has per-user and per-app rate limits; high-volume file migration or bulk operations must implement exponential backoff to avoid throttling
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise content governance. SOC2, ISO27001, HIPAA, FedRAMP, GxP. OAuth2. US/EU. File and PHI data. Regulated industry focus.

security_score_components.security_notes: Content governance. SOC2, ISO27001, HIPAA, FedRAMP. OAuth2. US/EU. File and regulated data.
